parameters.name: timepreroll
parameters.label: Preroll Time
parameters.description: How many seconds of the simulation to bypass, after the reset time is reached. For example, if you put the number 33 into this field (and reset is at ), frame one will show the simulation that was at

parameters.name: timeinc
parameters.label: Time Inc
parameters.description: The parameter determines how often to cook the . By default, this parameter is set to . This means that the will cook once for every frame. When complex dynamics are involved, the may require more fre

parameters.name: accurate
parameters.label: Accurate Moves
parameters.description: This option makes the nodes move more accurately between frames by calculating their trajectories for fractional frame values.

parameters.name: attractmode
parameters.label: Attractor Use
parameters.description: Describes how attractor points are assigned to each particle.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: While On resets the spring effect of the .

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly reset the spring effect.

parameters.name: period
parameters.label: Turb Period
parameters.description: A small period means that the turbulence varies quickly over a small area, while a larger value will cause points close to each other to be affected similarly.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Random number seed for the simulation.

parameters.name: fixed
parameters.label: Fixed Points
parameters.description: This is a point group. All points in the point group will remain unaffected by the forces. Also see the for notes on how to specify point ranges.

parameters.name: revertfixed
parameters.label: Fixed Points go to Source Positions
parameters.description: Determines whether or not points in the group should be moved to the positions of the corresponding points in the Source geometry.

parameters.name: copygroups
parameters.label: Copy Groups from Source
parameters.description: Determines if the Spring should copy groups from the Source geometry at each frame. This lets you specify the name of an animating group in the field, and the contents of this group will be kept up to

parameters.name: domass
parameters.label: Add Mass Attribute
parameters.description: When selected, the is computed for the deforming geometry.

parameters.name: mass
parameters.label: Mass
parameters.description: of each point. Heavier points take longer to get into motion, and longer to stop.

parameters.name: dodrag
parameters.label: Add Drag Attribute
parameters.description: When selected, the geometry is deformed by the attribute.

parameters.name: drag
parameters.label: Drag
parameters.description: Drag of each point.

parameters.name: springbehavior
parameters.label: Spring Behavior
parameters.description: How the springs will behave:

parameters.name: springk
parameters.label: Spring Constant
parameters.description: The spring constant. How tight the springs are. Increase this value to make the springs tighter and thus make the object more rigid. As this number becomes higher, the springs can oscillate out of con

parameters.name: tension
parameters.label: Initial Tension
parameters.description: The Initial k constant of the geometry before being deformed by the spring operation.

parameters.name: hit
parameters.label: Hit Behavior
parameters.description: Control over what happens when the geometry hits either the six collision planes or the collision object. The options are:

parameters.name: gaintan
parameters.label: Gain Tangent
parameters.description: Friction parameters which can be regarded as energy-loss upon collision. The first parameter affects the energy loss (gain) perpendicular to the surface. 0 means all energy (velocity) is lost, 1 means

parameters.name: gainnorm
parameters.label: Gain Normal
parameters.description: Friction parameters which can be regarded as energy-loss upon collision. The first parameter affects the energy loss (gain) perpendicular to the surface. 0 means all energy (velocity) is lost, 1 means

parameters.name: windz
parameters.label: Windz
parameters.description: Wind forces acting on the points. Similar to external force. Using wind (and no other forces, such as turbulence), the points will not exceed the wind velocity.

parameters.name: turbz
parameters.label: Turbz
parameters.description: The amplitude of turbulent (chaotic) forces along each axis. Use positive values, if any.

parameters.name: limitnegz
parameters.label: Limitnegz
parameters.description: The points will bounce off the limit planes when it reaches them. The six limit plane fields define a bounding cube. The default settings are one thousand units away, which is very large. Reduce the v

parameters.name: limitposz
parameters.label: Limitposz
parameters.description: The points will bounce off the limit planes when it reaches them. The six limit plane fields define a bounding cube. The default settings are one thousand units away, which is very large. Reduce the v

parameters.name: externalz
parameters.label: Externalz
parameters.description: Forces of gravity acting on the points. When drag is zero, the points can accelerate with no limit on their speed.
